message:
feat(fcp-core): add Display + as_str for BudgetStatus, pin contract via tests

Why
---
BudgetStatus is the connector- and zone-budget classifier carried
in UsageBudgetUsage::status snapshots, surfaced to operators in
budget reports, and embedded in serialized usage records. Until now
it relied on Debug for human display and on derived serde to
emit its wire form. Other variant enums in fcp-core
(OrderingPolicy, ConnectorRoute, ConnectorLifecycleState,
EventSeverity, etc.) follow a consistent pattern: an inherent
as_str() returning the canonical wire/display string, plus a
Display impl that delegates to it. Bringing BudgetStatus in
line with that pattern unblocks the next round of bead-driven test
pins for the budget subsystem and gives one canonical formatting
path for log lines, error messages, and serde tag derivation.

The accompanying test pins the externally-visible contract: that
BudgetStatus::Ok and BudgetStatus::Exceeded always render to "ok"
and "exceeded" respectively, that the JSON wire form is exactly
"ok" / "exceeded", and that the CBOR encoding is the text
major-type of the same string. This makes future drift (say,
somebody renaming a variant or accidentally retagging serde)
caught by the test rather than slipping through to operator-facing
budget reports.

What
----
- crates/fcp-core/src/policy.rs:
  - New inherent BudgetStatus::as_str(self) -> &'static str
    returning "ok" / "exceeded". const-callable so it can
    be used in lookup arrays and const contexts.
  - New impl std::fmt::Display for BudgetStatus that writes
    self.as_str() directly via f.write_str.

- crates/fcp-core/tests/connector_budget_display.rs (new, 97
  lines): pins BudgetStatus Display + serde tag for both
  variants. Closes the bead asking for a ConnectorBudget Display
  + serde tag pin (note: no public type literally named
  ConnectorBudget exists in fcp-core; BudgetStatus is the
  connector-facing budget classifier).

Compatibility
-------------
Pure addition. The wire form for BudgetStatus was already
lowercase "ok" / "exceeded" via #[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
on the enum, so the new as_str and Display return exactly the
strings already on the wire. Callers that previously Debug-printed
the variant will see the new lowercase form when they migrate to
Display; Debug itself is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
